content.text: What Policy Scope and Exclusions Control

Every policy assignment has a scope — the ARM hierarchy level at which the policy applies and from which it inherits downward. Within that scope, Azure Policy evaluates every resource and reports compliance. Two mechanisms narrow the set of resources actually evaluated: notScopes (a property on the assignment that completely excludes sub-scopes from evaluation) and Exemptions (a separate resource that excludes specific resources from enforcement while keeping them in compliance reporting). Choosing between them — or whether to use either — depends on whether you need the exclusion to show up in compliance data.

attrs.alt: Policy scope, notScopes exclusions, and exemptions diagram

content.text: The Assignment Scope Property

The scope property on a policy assignment is the ARM resource path at which the assignment is anchored. The assignment evaluates every resource at that scope or below, subject to notScopes and exemptions. Valid scope values are management group, subscription, resource group, or individual resource ARM paths.

# Assign at management group — evaluates all subscriptions, resource groups, and resources below
az policy assignment create \
  --name require-cost-center \
  --scope /providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/mg-corp \
  --policy $DEF_ID

# Assign at subscription — evaluates all RGs and resources in that subscription
az policy assignment create \
  --name require-cost-center \
  --scope /subscriptions/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111 \
  --policy $DEF_ID

# Assign at resource group — only evaluates resources inside that RG
az policy assignment create \
  --name require-cost-center \
  --scope /subscriptions/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111/resourceGroups/rg-prod \
  --policy $DEF_ID

# Show the scope of an existing assignment
az policy assignment show \
  --name require-cost-center \
  --scope /subscriptions/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111 \
  --query '{Scope:scope,Enforce:enforcementMode}' -o json
notScopes: Permanent Sub-Scope Exclusions

The notScopes property is an array of ARM scope strings that are permanently excluded from the assignment's evaluation. Resources under a notScope path are never evaluated — they produce no compliance events, no audit records, and do not appear in compliance reports for that assignment. This is appropriate for entirely unmanaged environments like sandbox subscriptions or legacy resource groups that will never be brought into compliance.
notScopes syntax and rules
Must be child scopes of the assignment's scope — you cannot notScope something outside the assignment scope
Supports subscription, resource group, and resource-level paths as exclusions
Wildcards are NOT supported — each excluded path must be an exact ARM scope string
Changes to notScopes require updating the assignment (not a separate resource), so they carry no audit trail beyond ARM activity logs
Resources in a notScope path will not trigger DINE remediation tasks or Modify operations

# Assign at MG scope, exclude sandbox and legacy subscriptions from evaluation
az policy assignment create \
  --name require-tags \
  --scope /providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/mg-corp \
  --policy $TAG_POLICY_ID \
  --not-scopes \
    "/subscriptions/aaaaaaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaaaa" \
    "/subscriptions/bbbbbbbb-bbbb-bbbb-bbbb-bbbbbbbbbbbb/resourceGroups/rg-legacy" \
    "/subscriptions/bbbbbbbb-bbbb-bbbb-bbbb-bbbbbbbbbbbb/resourceGroups/rg-demo"

# Update notScopes on an existing assignment (add a new exclusion)
az policy assignment update \
  --name require-tags \
  --scope /providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/mg-corp \
  --not-scopes \
    "/subscriptions/aaaaaaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaa-aaaaaaaaaaaa" \
    "/subscriptions/bbbbbbbb-bbbb-bbbb-bbbb-bbbbbbbbbbbb/resourceGroups/rg-legacy" \
    "/subscriptions/bbbbbbbb-bbbb-bbbb-bbbb-bbbbbbbbbbbb/resourceGroups/rg-demo" \
    "/subscriptions/cccccccc-cccc-cccc-cccc-cccccccccccc/resourceGroups/rg-shared-svc"

# Verify notScopes on assignment
az policy assignment show \
  --name require-tags \
  --scope /providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/mg-corp \
  --query 'notScopes' -o json

Adding a path to notScopes is an all-or-nothing exclusion — the entire subtree is dropped from evaluation. If you need to exclude a specific resource while keeping its siblings evaluated, use an Exemption instead. notScopes cannot target individual resources within a resource group.
Exemptions: Tracked Exclusions with Audit Trail

An Exemption is a separate Azure resource (type Microsoft.Authorization/policyExemptions) that marks a specific resource or scope as excluded from a policy assignment's effect while still recording compliance state. Unlike notScopes, exemptions appear in compliance reports (as 'Exempt' state), support expiration dates, require a justification category, and can be time-limited. They are the correct mechanism for business-justified exceptions that need governance visibility.
Exemption categories
Category
Meaning
When to Use
Waiver
The policy requirement is waived for this resource — the risk is accepted
Risk accepted by business: legacy system, cost-prohibitive remediation, architectural constraint
Mitigated
The intent of the policy is satisfied by an alternative control
Compensating control exists: network-isolated resource with firewall instead of private endpoint, third-party security agent instead of Azure Defender

# Create a Waiver exemption for a specific storage account
# The storage account is evaluated but shown as Exempt, not Non-Compliant
az policy exemption create \
  --name sa-compliance-data-waiver \
  --display-name 'Compliance data export SA — HTTPS-only waiver' \
  --scope /subscriptions/$SUB_ID/resourceGroups/rg-prod/providers/Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/sa-compliance-data \
  --policy-assignment /subscriptions/$SUB_ID/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/require-https \
  --exemption-category Waiver \
  --description 'Compliance data export pipeline requires HTTP for legacy SIEM connector. Mitigated by VNet isolation. PAR-2024-147.' \
  --expires-on 2025-06-30T00:00:00Z

# Create a Mitigated exemption for a Key Vault
az policy exemption create \
  --name kv-legacy-api-mitigated \
  --display-name 'kv-legacy-api — private endpoint mitigated by firewall rules' \
  --scope /subscriptions/$SUB_ID/resourceGroups/rg-api/providers/Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/kv-legacy-api \
  --policy-assignment /subscriptions/$SUB_ID/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/require-private-endpoint \
  --exemption-category Mitigated \
  --description 'KV is firewall-restricted to APIM subnet only. Full private endpoint migration planned for Q4 2024. CAB-2024-089.' \
  --expires-on 2024-12-31T00:00:00Z

# List all exemptions in a subscription
az policy exemption list \
  --scope /subscriptions/$SUB_ID \
  --query '[].{Name:name,Display:displayName,Category:exemptionCategory,Expires:expiresOn}' \
  -o table

# Check for expired exemptions (resources may re-enter non-compliant state after expiry)
az policy exemption list \
  --scope /subscriptions/$SUB_ID \
  --query "[?expiresOn < '$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)'].{Name:name,ExpiresOn:expiresOn,Resource:id}" \
  -o table
notScopes vs Exemptions: Decision Guide
Factor
Use notScopes
Use Exemption
Compliance visibility
No — resource invisible to reporting
Yes — resource shown as Exempt in compliance reports
Expiration
No — permanent until assignment is updated
Yes — expiresOn field auto-expires the exemption
Granularity
Subscription or resource group only
Any ARM resource, RG, or subscription
Audit trail
ARM activity log only (assignment update)
Dedicated policy exemption resource with metadata and category
Initiative exemption
Excludes from all policies in the initiative
Can target specific policy definitions within an initiative
Governance review
Hard to discover; requires assignment inspection
Listable via az policy exemption list; searchable in Resource Graph
Justification required
No field for justification
description field + category (Waiver/Mitigated) required by convention

Prefer Exemptions over notScopes in all cases where you need governance visibility. notScopes should be reserved for environments that are completely outside the governance boundary (sandbox subscriptions, disconnected dev environments) where producing zero compliance data is intentional.
Initiative-Scoped Exemptions

When an initiative (policy set) assignment is used instead of an individual policy assignment, an Exemption can target either the entire initiative or specific policy definition references within it. This lets you exempt a resource from one policy within an initiative while keeping all other policies in that initiative enforced.

# Exempt a VM from a specific policy definition within an initiative assignment
# The initiative 'corp-security-baseline' contains multiple policies.
# We only want to exempt this VM from the 'require-disk-encryption' policy within it.

# First, find the policy definition reference IDs inside the initiative
az policy set-definition show \
  --name corp-security-baseline \
  --management-group mg-corp \
  --query 'policyDefinitions[].{RefId:policyDefinitionReferenceId,Name:policyDefinitionId}' \
  -o table

# Create a partial exemption targeting only one policy within the initiative
az policy exemption create \
  --name vm-db-01-encryption-waiver \
  --display-name 'vm-db-01: disk encryption waiver — SQL managed disk with TDE' \
  --scope /subscriptions/$SUB_ID/resourceGroups/rg-db/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/vm-db-01 \
  --policy-assignment /subscriptions/$SUB_ID/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/corp-security-baseline \
  --exemption-category Mitigated \
  --policy-definition-reference-ids 'requireDiskEncryptionRefId' \
  --description 'SQL VM uses TDE (Transparent Data Encryption) as compensating control. CAB-2024-112.'

# Verify the exemption is initiative-specific
az policy exemption show \
  --name vm-db-01-encryption-waiver \
  --scope /subscriptions/$SUB_ID/resourceGroups/rg-db/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/vm-db-01 \
  --query '{PolicyAssignment:policyAssignmentId,PolicyRefs:policyDefinitionReferenceIds}'
Terraform: Managing Exemptions as Code

Terraform's azurerm_management_group_policy_exemption, azurerm_subscription_policy_exemption, and azurerm_resource_group_policy_exemption resources create exemptions declaratively with full lifecycle management. Storing exemptions as code ensures they are peer-reviewed, auditable via Git history, and automatically recreated if deleted out of band.

# policy-exemptions.tf

variable "policy_assignment_id" { type = string }
variable "sub_id" { type = string }

# Waiver: storage account in legacy RG requires HTTP for compliance data export
resource "azurerm_resource_policy_exemption" "sa_compliance_waiver" {
  name                 = "sa-compliance-data-waiver"
  display_name         = "Compliance data SA: HTTPS-only waiver"
  exemption_category   = "Waiver"
  policy_assignment_id = var.policy_assignment_id
  resource_id          = "/subscriptions/${var.sub_id}/resourceGroups/rg-prod/providers/Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/sa-compliance-data"
  description          = "Compliance data export pipeline requires HTTP for legacy SIEM connector. VNet isolated. PAR-2024-147."
  expires_on           = "2025-06-30T00:00:00Z"
}

# Mitigated: Key Vault uses firewall rules instead of private endpoint
resource "azurerm_resource_policy_exemption" "kv_legacy_mitigated" {
  name                 = "kv-legacy-api-mitigated"
  display_name         = "kv-legacy-api: private endpoint mitigated"
  exemption_category   = "Mitigated"
  policy_assignment_id = var.policy_assignment_id
  resource_id          = "/subscriptions/${var.sub_id}/resourceGroups/rg-api/providers/Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/kv-legacy-api"
  description          = "KV firewall-restricted to APIM subnet only. Full PE migration in Q4 2024. CAB-2024-089."
  expires_on           = "2024-12-31T00:00:00Z"

  # Partial exemption: only exempt from the private-endpoint policy within the initiative
  policy_definition_reference_ids = ["requirePrivateEndpointForKeyVault"]
}

# Subscription-level notScopes via assignment update (Terraform replaces the whole assignment)
# Use azurerm_subscription_policy_assignment with not_scopes array
resource "azurerm_management_group_policy_assignment" "corp_require_tags" {
  name                 = "corp-require-tags"
  display_name         = "Corp: Require cost-center tag"
  policy_definition_id = var.tag_policy_def_id
  management_group_id  = var.corp_mg_id

  not_scopes = [
    # Sandbox subscriptions — entirely excluded, no compliance data generated
    "/subscriptions/${var.sandbox_sub_id}",
    "/subscriptions/${var.demo_sub_id}",
  ]
}

Terraform destroys and recreates azurerm_resource_policy_exemption if the scope changes (because the exemption ID is scope-anchored). To move an exemption to a new resource scope, import the new exemption after creating it rather than relying on Terraform's in-place update.
Querying Scope and Exemption State with Resource Graph

// Find all active (non-expired) policy exemptions in the tenant
PolicyResources
| where type =~ 'Microsoft.Authorization/policyExemptions'
| extend expiresOn = tostring(properties.expiresOn)
| where isempty(expiresOn) or datetime(expiresOn) > now()
| project
    name,
    scope = tostring(properties.scope),
    category = tostring(properties.exemptionCategory),
    policyAssignment = tostring(properties.policyAssignmentId),
    description = tostring(properties.description),
    expiresOn
| order by expiresOn asc nulls last

// Find exemptions expiring in the next 30 days (remediation window)
PolicyResources
| where type =~ 'Microsoft.Authorization/policyExemptions'
| extend expiresOn = todatetime(properties.expiresOn)
| where isnotnull(expiresOn)
| where expiresOn between (now() .. now()+30d)
| project name, scope = tostring(properties.scope), expiresOn, category = tostring(properties.exemptionCategory)
| order by expiresOn asc

// Show resources by compliance state (Compliant / NonCompliant / Exempt / Unknown)
PolicyResources
| where type =~ 'Microsoft.PolicyInsights/PolicyStates'
| summarize Count = count() by complianceState = tostring(properties.complianceState)
| order by Count desc

// Find non-compliant resources NOT covered by any exemption
// These are the resources that genuinely need remediation
PolicyResources
| where type =~ 'Microsoft.PolicyInsights/PolicyStates'
| where properties.complianceState == 'NonCompliant'
| project
    resourceId = tostring(properties.resourceId),
    policyAssignmentName = tostring(properties.policyAssignmentName),
    policyDefinitionAction = tostring(properties.policyDefinitionAction),
    subscriptionId
| order by policyDefinitionAction desc, policyAssignmentName asc
Compliance Impact of Scope Choices

The choice of assignment scope directly affects the compliance percentage shown in Azure Policy dashboards and sent to Microsoft Defender for Cloud secure score. A policy assigned at MG scope with a broad notScopes list will show higher compliance than one assigned at subscription scope without notScopes — even if the underlying resource set is identical. Understanding this helps interpret compliance numbers accurately and avoid misreporting.

# Trigger a compliance scan to get current state after scope changes
az policy state trigger-scan \
  --subscription $SUB_ID

# Get compliance summary for a specific assignment
az policy state summarize \
  --subscription $SUB_ID \
  --filter "policyAssignmentId eq '/subscriptions/$SUB_ID/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/require-tags'" \
  --query 'value[0].results.{Compliant:compliantResources,NonCompliant:nonCompliantResources,Exempt:exemptResources}'

# Compare compliance rates before/after adding notScopes
# Before: 847 compliant, 120 non-compliant -> 87.6% compliant
# After adding notScopes for legacy RG (100 non-compliant resources): 847 compliant, 20 non-compliant -> 97.7% compliant
# But those 100 resources are now invisible to compliance reporting

# Get compliance state for a specific resource
az policy state list \
  --resource $RESOURCE_ID \
  --query '[].{Assignment:policyAssignmentName,State:complianceState,Effect:policyDefinitionAction}' \
  -o table
Exemption vs notScopes in compliance numbers
Metric
notScopes
Exemption
Compliance %
Excluded from denominator — boosts %
Counted as Exempt — not in numerator or denominator of pass/fail
Defender for Cloud Secure Score
Resources invisible — not penalized
Resources excluded from score calculation (same effect as notScopes)
Compliance report export
No entry for excluded resources
Entry with 'Exempt' state and exemption category
Audit log
No evaluation events generated
PolicyStateChanged events show 'Exempt' transition
Regulatory compliance view
Control shows as passing (no failures)
Control shows Exempt resources separately from compliant/non-compliant
Scope and Exemption Lifecycle Management

Exemptions with expiry dates become active governance debt if not reviewed before they expire. A resource that was legitimately exempt under a time-bound exception silently re-enters non-compliant state at expiry — without notifying the resource team. A systematic review process prevents surprise compliance failures at audit time.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# exemption-review.sh — weekly governance review of expiring exemptions
SUB='11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111'

echo '=== Exemptions Expiring in Next 14 Days ==='
CUTOFF=$(date -u -d '+14 days' +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ 2>/dev/null || date -u -v+14d +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
az policy exemption list \
  --scope /subscriptions/$SUB \
  --query "[?expiresOn != null && expiresOn < '$CUTOFF'].{Name:name,Display:displayName,Expires:expiresOn,Category:exemptionCategory}" \
  -o table

echo ''
echo '=== Already Expired Exemptions (should be deleted or renewed) ==='
NOW=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
az policy exemption list \
  --scope /subscriptions/$SUB \
  --query "[?expiresOn != null && expiresOn < '$NOW'].{Name:name,Display:displayName,Expires:expiresOn}" \
  -o table

echo ''
echo '=== Exemptions with No Expiry (permanent waivers — review annually) ==='
az policy exemption list \
  --scope /subscriptions/$SUB \
  --query "[?expiresOn == null].{Name:name,Display:displayName,Category:exemptionCategory,Description:description}" \
  -o table

echo ''
echo '=== Total Exemption Count ==='
az policy exemption list --scope /subscriptions/$SUB --query 'length(@)' -o tsv

# Renew an expiring exemption by updating the expiresOn date
# (Requires justification review — do not auto-renew without approval)
az policy exemption update \
  --name sa-compliance-data-waiver \
  --scope /subscriptions/$SUB_ID/resourceGroups/rg-prod/providers/Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/sa-compliance-data \
  --expires-on 2026-06-30T00:00:00Z \
  --description 'Renewed: SIEM connector upgrade delayed to H2 2025. PAR-2025-042.'

# Delete an exemption when the underlying issue is resolved
az policy exemption delete \
  --name kv-legacy-api-mitigated \
  --scope /subscriptions/$SUB_ID/resourceGroups/rg-api/providers/Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/kv-legacy-api

# Delete all expired exemptions in a subscription (dry-run first with --dry-run pattern)
NOW=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
az policy exemption list \
  --scope /subscriptions/$SUB_ID \
  --query "[?expiresOn != null && expiresOn < '$NOW'].{name:name,scope:id}" \
  -o json | jq -r '.[] | "az policy exemption delete --name " + .name + " --scope " + .scope' > delete-expired-exemptions.sh
cat delete-expired-exemptions.sh  # Review before executing
# bash delete-expired-exemptions.sh  # Execute after review
notScopes Pitfalls and Edge Cases
A child scope cannot be added to notScopes if it is outside the assignment's scope — Azure returns a validation error: the exclusion must be a descendant of the assignment scope.
Resource-level notScopes are not supported — only management group, subscription, and resource group paths are valid notScopes values. Use an Exemption to exclude a specific resource.

Removing a scope from notScopes immediately subjects it to evaluation — resources in the previously excluded scope will appear as non-compliant if they violate the policy. Plan the removal with a DoNotEnforce grace period.
notScopes is evaluated at request time for deny/append/modify effects — if a resource is in a notScope, the ARM request is allowed through even if the policy would otherwise deny it.

A subscription's resources are not visible in compliance reporting while the subscription is in a notScope — this can create gaps in Defender for Cloud coverage that are not obvious without explicit auditing.

# Detect resources that were in notScopes and are now re-exposed
# After removing a scope from notScopes, trigger a scan and check new non-compliant resources

# Step 1: Record current non-compliant count for the assignment
BEFORE=$(az policy state summarize \
  --subscription $SUB_ID \
  --filter "policyAssignmentId eq '/subscriptions/$SUB_ID/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/require-tags'" \
  --query 'value[0].results.nonCompliantResources' -o tsv)
echo "Non-compliant before notScopes update: $BEFORE"

# Step 2: Update the assignment to remove the scope from notScopes
az policy assignment update \
  --name require-tags \
  --scope /subscriptions/$SUB_ID \
  --not-scopes ''  # Remove all notScopes

# Step 3: Trigger re-evaluation
az policy state trigger-scan --subscription $SUB_ID

# Step 4: Check new non-compliant count (may be significantly higher)
AFTER=$(az policy state summarize \
  --subscription $SUB_ID \
  --filter "policyAssignmentId eq '/subscriptions/$SUB_ID/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments/require-tags'" \
  --query 'value[0].results.nonCompliantResources' -o tsv)
echo "Non-compliant after notScopes update: $AFTER"
echo "New non-compliant resources exposed: $((AFTER - BEFORE))"
Summary

Policy scope determines which resources an assignment evaluates. notScopes permanently removes sub-scopes from evaluation with no compliance data generated — appropriate for sandbox or legacy environments outside the governance boundary. Exemptions are the preferred mechanism for business-justified exceptions: they remain visible in compliance reports, support expiration dates, require a justification category, and create an auditable record separate from the assignment itself. For initiative assignments, partial exemptions can target specific policy definition references within the initiative rather than exempting the resource from all policies at once. Managing exemptions as Terraform code ensures they are peer-reviewed, version-controlled, and not silently created outside the governance process.
